topic: Indian activist forcibly removed by police during hunger strike
headline: SONAM WANGCHUK FORCIBLY REMOVED BY POLICE DURING 18-DAY HUNGER STRIKE AT JANTAR MANTAR OVER NEET DEMANDS
summary: Indian climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly removed by police during his hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, which had entered its 18th day. Wangchuk had been demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in the NEET exam. Despite growing concerns over his health, Wangchuk had continued his strike, with many voicing their support for him. The hunger strike was part of his protest for various causes, including autonomy for the people of Ladakh. Wangchuk's health had been deteriorating during the strike.
